Migrating From Heroku to Render: A Deployment Guide

Heroku was my go-to backend platform for years, but in 2022/11/28 they killed the free tier. This post is the walkthrough I went through migrating my sites to Render.com — why I picked Render, how to deploy a Node.js project end to end (with environment variables), and the Render-specific quirks worth knowing about (sleep mode, shared monthly hours, slow free-tier deploys, the HTTP endpoint requirement, and bots that send twice).

Build Your First LINE Bot with Node.js — Using OpenAI GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 as Examples

OpenAI's ChatGPT has been blowing up recently, and OpenAI also exposes an API we can integrate with. This post focuses on GPT-3 but also covers how to switch to ChatGPT API. We'll use Node.js to build a LINE chatbot backed by OpenAI.
Buy a Domain (Google Domains) and Point It at Your Blog on GitHub Pages With Cloudflare

Heads up — Google Domains was discontinued in 2023 and transferred to Squarespace. The Google Domains-specific parts of this post are historical, but the GitHub Pages + Cloudflare setup is still applicable. If you're setting up a new domain today, see my newer post on Cloudflare Domain instead. Keeping this post as historical reference for anyone who already has a Google Domain.
